summary: Matched the common fighter ledge jump implementation by moving the ftCo_CliffJump routines out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new C source/header pair. The PR added ftCo_CliffJump.c to obj_files.mk, exposed the cliff jump callbacks and helpers through ftCo_CliffJump.h, added a cliffjump motion-var struct at fp+2340, and tightened nearby prototypes needed by the C implementation. The matching report shows the new file and all listed functions at 100.00%. No review comments were present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Deleted the matched ftCo_CliffJump assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, including ftCo_8009B170, ftCo_8009B1B8, ftCo_CliffJump1_Anim, ftCo_CliffJump1_IASA, ftCo_CliffJump1_Phys, ftCo_CliffJump1_Coll, ftCo_8009B2F8, ft_8009B390, ftCo_CliffJump2_Anim, ftCo_CliffJump2_IASA, ftCo_CliffJump2_Phys, and ftCo_CliffJump2_Coll.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffJump.c implementing the cliff jump entry check, first-stage cliff jump callbacks, transition into second-stage cliff jump, forced second-stage helper ft_8009B390, second-stage animation/physics/collision callbacks, and static internal helpers.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffJump.h with prototypes for the exported cliff jump routines and address comments using the GALE01_09B170 include guard style.
Updated obj_files.mk so ftCo_CliffJump.c.o is built with the other ftCommon cliff action objects, before the remaining ft_08A4.s.o assembly object.
Updated ftCo_CliffWait.c to include ftCo_CliffJump.h, replacing reliance on broader or old ft_08A4 declarations for cliff jump routines.
Added ftCommon_MotionVars::cliffjump with a bool x0 at fp+2340, matching the second-stage cliff jump physics state used by ftCo_CliffJump2_Phys and reset by ftCo_8009B2F8/ft_8009B390.
Changed ft_800CAE80's declaration in ft_0877.h from s32 to bool, matching its use as a boolean condition in ftCo_8009B170.

Typed ft_800835B0 in ft_081B.c and ft_081B.h as void ft_800835B0(Fighter_GObj* gobj, bool (*)(Fighter_GObj*, enum_t), HSD_GObjEvent), matching ftCo_CliffJump2_Coll's call with ft_80096CC8 and ft_80082B1C.
Removed obsolete cliff jump declarations from src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h, according to the changed-file summary.
smart_moves: Kept the new C file narrowly scoped to the cliff jump action, mirroring nearby ftCommon cliff action organization such as ftCo_CliffClimb, ftCo_CliffAttack, and ftCo_CliffEscape.
Used a dedicated ftCo_CliffJump.h and included it from ftCo_CliffWait.c, which localizes cliff jump declarations instead of continuing to route them through ft_08A4.h.

Modeled the fp+2340 motion-variable access as fp->mv.co.cliffjump.x0 rather than leaving it as a raw offset, making the two-frame/first-physics-tick behavior in ftCo_CliffJump2_Phys understandable and reusable.
Converted known boolean-returning/boolean-consuming APIs to concrete bool prototypes, specifically ft_800CAE80 and the callback parameter of ft_800835B0.
Preserved matching where needed with MUST_MATCH-only unused stack arrays in ftCo_8009B170, ftCo_8009B2F8, and ft_8009B390, rather than polluting normal builds unconditionally.
Encoded motion-state selection with FtMotionId and named ftCo_MS_CliffJumpQuick1/Slow1/Quick2/Slow2 values, clarifying the original immediate constants 0x104 through 0x107.

Kept trivial callbacks as empty C functions for IASA and direct wrappers for shared physics/collision helpers, accurately reflecting the original branch-to-helper behavior while improving readability.
decomp_lessons: When moving an action from an umbrella asm file into ftCommon C, also update obj_files.mk so the new C object is linked before the remaining asm object at the same position in TEXT_O_FILES.
Motion variable fields at fp+2340 should be represented through ftCommon_MotionVars when the action owns the storage; this avoids raw offset use and documents per-action state.
Common fighter action callbacks should use the ftCo_GObj/Fighter_GObj typedefs consistently and include the action-specific header from neighboring action files that call them.
If an asm routine uses integer constants for motion IDs, prefer mapping them to FtMotionId enum names once the relationship is clear from surrounding action state.
MUST_MATCH stack padding can be isolated behind #ifdef MUST_MATCH with a TODO when an otherwise clean C implementation needs unused stack space to match.
Prototype cleanup is often required for matching newly decompiled action code: unknown prototypes can block readable calls or force casts, while typed callbacks expose intended control flow.
Empty IASA callbacks and one-line physics/collision callbacks are valid decomp outputs when they correspond to blr-only or direct-helper assembly routines.
naming_conventions: Action file/header names follow ftCo_<Action>.c and ftCo_<Action>.h, here ftCo_CliffJump.c and ftCo_CliffJump.h.
Common action callbacks use the pattern ftCo_CliffJump1_Anim, ftCo_CliffJump1_IASA, ftCo_CliffJump1_Phys, ftCo_CliffJump1_Coll and the same pattern for CliffJump2.
Still-unknown helper names remained address-based, such as ftCo_8009B170, ftCo_8009B1B8, ftCo_8009B2F8, and ft_8009B390, rather than inventing semantic names.
Header prototypes include address comments like /* 09B170 */ before declarations.
The new header used the existing address-derived include guard style, GALE01_09B170.
Motion IDs use ftCo_MS_CliffJumpQuick1, ftCo_MS_CliffJumpSlow1, ftCo_MS_CliffJumpQuick2, and ftCo_MS_CliffJumpSlow2.
The per-action motion variable is named mv.co.cliffjump.x0 in the absence of stronger semantic evidence for the field.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced roughly 271 deleted assembly lines in ft_08A4.s with 117 lines of C while preserving all listed function matches at 100.00%.
Used float comparison against p_ftCommonData->x488 and fp->dmg.x1830_percent to reproduce the quick-versus-slow first cliff jump motion-state branch.
Used Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, msid, Ft_MF_None, NULL, 0, 1, 0) followed by ftAnim_8006EBA4(gobj) to match the motion transition sequences.
Called ftCommon_8007E2F4(fp, 32), set fp->x221D_b7 = true, and then called ftCo_CliffCatch_Phys(gobj) in the first cliff jump setup to reproduce the original side effects.
Transitioned from CliffJump1 to CliffJump2 only when ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) returned false.
Reset fp->mv.co.cliffjump.x0 to false during transition into CliffJump2, then in ftCo_CliffJump2_Phys set it true on the first call and called ft_80084DB0 on later calls.

Matched horizontal and vertical ledge-jump velocity setup via fp->self_vel.x += fp->facing_dir * fp->co_attrs.ledge_jump_horizontal_velocity and fp->self_vel.y = fp->co_attrs.ledge_jump_vertical_velocity.
Matched the alternate ft_8009B390 helper by applying force_mul only to ledge_jump_vertical_velocity and not reproducing the horizontal velocity addition present in ftCo_8009B2F8.
Matched second-stage collision by calling ft_800835B0(gobj, ft_80096CC8, ft_80082B1C).
Removed associated sdata2 constants from the asm file when the C translation generated or owned the needed constants.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text evidence is the matching report, which reports src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffJump.c and every listed function at 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_8009B170, ftCo_8009B1B8, ftCo_8009B2F8, or ft_8009B390 to semantic cliff jump helper names.
Search for later refinements to ftCommon_MotionVars::cliffjump.x0 to determine the actual meaning of the fp+2340 flag.
Look for PRs around ftCo_CliffClimb, ftCo_CliffAttack, and ftCo_CliffEscape for shared ledge-action matching patterns.
Check whether ft_800835B0 and ft_800CAE80 receive more descriptive names or fuller prototypes in later decomp work.
Search for other MUST_MATCH unused stack padding examples in fighter action files to reuse the same approach.
